title: transformItems function has issue with the key name "type"
body:
## 🐛 Bug description

The transformItems function fails silently when using the key _**type**_.  It also appears to prevent any other transforms from happening.

## 🔍 Bug reproduction

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**

1. Set up the transformItems function as below:
```php
transformItems(items) {
  return items.map(item => ({
    ...item,
    type: item.name.toUpperCase(),
  }));
}
```
or 
```php
transformItems(items) {
  return items.map(item => ({
    ...item,
    type: "Custom Type",
  }));
}
```
2. Create a template using hit.type as a value.
3. In the screenshot below I used the search phrase "District of Columbia Imposes Significant Restrictions on the Hospitality". If you need more results you can use "Background check requirements" as a search term. I've linked a sandbox for testing below.

My workaround was to change the key _**type**_ to _**custom_content_type_key**_ both in the transformItems function and in the result template.

<!-- A live example helps a lot! Fork the sandbox, reproduce the bug and paste the URL here: -->

**Live reproduction:**

https://codesandbox.io/s/elated-banach-02mqd?file=/src/app.js

## 💭 Expected behavior

<!-- A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. -->
That using  _type_ as a key in tranformItems would either transform the item or fail with an error.

## 🖥 Screenshots

<!-- If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem. -->
![Screen Shot 2021-08-14 at 2 12 02 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/282426/129458055-51b7ac67-6422-46a3-b82a-6524600d142a.png)

The screenshot above is with the _type_ parameter replaced with _custom_content_type_key_.


![Screen Shot 2021-08-14 at 2 26 24 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/282426/129458136-b7715769-beff-46ff-ac6c-1b8d17d16570.png)

The screenshot above uses the _type_ parameter. Notice the News & Analysis label (type) is missing from the left of the title. Also the date is missing to the left of the description.

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome
- Version: 92.0.4515.131

## Additional context
**Note:**
I'm using a Drupal module the passes along the content type of a record with the key _**type**_.
